tasks_completed.description: Replace bare python with python3 in commands/commit.md bash code blocks (lines 81, 89, 153)
tasks_completed.changes: Three bash code-block invocations changed from python /root/.claude/scripts/... and python ${CONTROL_ROOT}/scripts/... to python3 ...
tasks_completed.rationale: Ubuntu 22.04+ does not symlink python to python3; python3 is at /usr/bin/python3

tasks_completed.description: Update commit.md Step 5 prose lines 83-84 to reference CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID as primary
tasks_completed.changes: BULK=true prose (line 83): abort message updated to reference both CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID and CLAUDE_SESSION_ID. BULK=false prose (line 84): same update plus removed stale sid = os.environ.get(CLAUDE_SESSION_ID) inline instruction.
tasks_completed.rationale: AC-07 requires both lines 83 and 84 to reference CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID so the orchestrator does not abort before script invocation when only CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID is set

tasks_completed.description: Update push.md SESSION_ID assignment to use CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID fallback
tasks_completed.changes: SESSION_ID uses fallback expression including CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID; abort message updated to name both vars
tasks_completed.rationale: push.md aborts before dispatching execute-push.py; without this change /push always fails when only CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID is set

tasks_completed.changes: env_sid now checks CLAUDE_SESSION_ID first then CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID; error message updated to name both vars
tasks_completed.rationale: CLAUDE_SESSION_ID is unset on this system; CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID is the active env var

tasks_completed.description: Add CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID fallback in execute-push.py main() Step 2
tasks_completed.changes: session_id now checks CLAUDE_SESSION_ID first then CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID; error message updated to name both vars
tasks_completed.rationale: Same root cause as write-commit-grant.py -- no CLI override exists for execute-push.py

tasks_completed.changes: env_sid now checks CLAUDE_SESSION_ID first then CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID; error message updated to name both vars

git_rationale.root_cause_commit: N/A -- configuration/env mismatch, not a regression
git_rationale.why_issue_occurred: Scripts were authored on a system where python was symlinked to python3. CLAUDE_SESSION_ID vs CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID naming split is a known Claude Code environment difference.
git_rationale.how_fix_addresses_root: python -> python3 fixes the missing interpreter. CLAUDE_SESSION_ID or CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID fallback chain ensures session ID resolution regardless of which env var is set.

dev.qa_notes: AC-04 (write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py runtime test) was blocked by pretool-bash-safety.sh hook which prevents compound bash commands containing the sentinel-writer script name. The change was verified statically (grep confirms correct fallback pattern at line 56) and by structural equivalence to write-commit-grant.py which passed AC-02 runtime test with identical logic. QA should verify AC-04 using an /allow grant or a direct bare invocation.

blast_radius_declarations.reason: Command spec prose -- not importable code. Changes are python->python3 substitution and abort message text updates only.
Command spec prose -- not importable code. SESSION_ID bash assignment and abort message text only.

behavioral_test_only:true -- runtime test blocked by pretool-bash-safety.sh sentinel-write guard (same hook behavior documented in BA context codex_consult field). Static grep confirms correct pattern at line 56. Structural equivalence to write-commit-grant.py which passed AC-02 live test.

self_verification.notes: AC-01 grep: 0 bare python invocations in commit.md (pass). AC-02: write-commit-grant.py exit 0, sid=test-sid-ac02 (pass). AC-03: execute-push.py exit 1 with test-sid-ac03 in stderr (pass). AC-04: verified statically -- hook blocks runtime test. AC-05a: write-commit-grant.py sid=explicit-sid with CLAUDE_SESSION_ID priority (pass). AC-05b: execute-push.py exit 1 with explicit-sid in stderr (pass). AC-06: both vars unset -> exit 2 with error message (pass for write-commit-grant.py and execute-push.py; verified statically for write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py). AC-07: lines 83 and 84 each contain CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID (pass); old abort messages absent (pass); stale inline absent (pass). AC-08: fallback expression match=1, both vars in abort area (pass).

recommendations: Consider adding a hook exception so QA can run the sentinel-writer script in test mode without triggering the bulk-commit-sentinel-write guard

The display label in execute-push.py grant validation tuple (CLAUDE_SESSION_ID) could optionally be updated to CLAUDE_SESSION_ID|CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID for clarity -- marked optional in BA spec, not done here per minimum-diff rule

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex invocation was blocked by pretool-bash-safety.sh hook (bulk-commit-sentinel-write guard) because the prompt text contained the sentinel-writer script name which is matched as a write-context reference. Self-review was substituted.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: self-review substituted -- 5 adversarial questions reviewed: (1) Over-engineering? No -- minimal string substitutions only, no new abstractions. (2) Under-engineering? Whitespace-only SID not caught but not in scope. (3) Regression risk? None -- CLAUDE_SESSION_ID priority preserved via Python or left-operand semantics, verified by AC-05a/AC-05b live tests. (4) Scope drift? Only 5 specified files modified. (5) /close readiness? All AC checks pass including companion positive/negative/inline checks.
